name: Crown Roast of Pork with Sausage and Herb Stu
recipeIngredient: 1 Crown roast of pork, weighing about 6 lb
1/2 lb bulk sweet Italian sausage, crumbled
1/4 teaspoon chopped fresh thyme (or 1/8 teaspoon dried thyme)
1/4 teaspoon chopped fresh rosemary (or 1/8 teaspoon dried rosemary)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 400°F. Set a rack in the middle of the oven.
Place the crown roast on a rack in a roasting pan and season with salt and pepper.
Roast uncovered for 30 minutes on the middle rack.
Reduce heat to 325°F and continue roasting, basting frequently with pan juices, for another 45 minutes.
Sausage and Herb Stuffing:
In a large frying pan over medium-low heat, melt butter. Add onion and celery and sauté until soft, about 15 minutes.
Add garlic and sausage; cook, stirring, until sausage is browned, about 10 minutes longer.
Transfer the sausage mixture to a bowl using a slotted spoon; discard any remaining fat in the pan.
Add bread crumbs and egg to the sausage mixture. Add enough chicken stock to moisten the stuffing, then season with salt and pepper. Mix well.
Mound the stuffing in the center of the crown roast and return it to the oven.
Bake until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of the roast registers 150°F or a chop is pale pink when cut in the center (about 3 minutes longer). If needed, cover rib ends with foil if they brown before the meat is done.
Remove roast from the oven and cover loosely with foil. Let stand for 10 minutes before carving.
Carve the roast between the ribs. Serve each portion with a spoonful of stuffing.
Serves 6-8.
Herbed Bread Crumbs:
Use coarse-textured white bread that can be purchased sliced in 1- or 1-1/2-pound loaves.
Remove crusts and discard.
Tear bread into pieces; place in a food processor fitted with a metal blade or blender.
Process until the bread forms coarse crumbs. Add salt, pepper, thyme, and rosemary; pulse a few times just until well mixed.
Use immediately or store in an airtight container in the freezer for up to 1 year. This recipe makes about 1 cup; triple it for use with Sausage and Herb Stuffing.
